scoring.enso_fit: 0-10, higher = better fit for current Enso infrastructure
scoring.ai_leverage: 0-10, higher = more benefit from LLM/AI integration

strategies.name: IV vs RV Gap Monitor
strategies.description: Trades the gap between implied and realized volatility. When IV is rich relative to RV, sell premium via credit spreads. When IV is cheap, buy premium via debit spreads.
strategies.why_institutions_use_it: The IV-RV gap is one of the most persistent edges in options markets. Implied volatility systematically overestimates realized volatility, creating a harvestable premium.
strategies.enso_status: Ready to build — yfinance provides historical vol data, just need to add IV from options chains

strategies.name: Dealer Gamma Regime
strategies.description: Classifies positive/negative gamma regimes from dealer positioning. In positive gamma, markets mean-revert intraday. In negative gamma, moves accelerate.
strategies.why_institutions_use_it: Dealer hedging flows mechanically move markets. Understanding whether dealers are long or short gamma tells you whether to fade moves or ride them.
strategies.enso_status: Requires GEX data subscription — SpotGamma or SqueezeMetrics

strategies.name: Skew Surface Trading
strategies.description: Trades mispricings across strike, tenor, and smile shape. Buys relatively cheap wings and sells rich ones when the surface is inconsistent.
strategies.why_institutions_use_it: Vol surface mispricings are persistent because different participants (hedgers, speculators, market makers) create systematic distortions.
strategies.enso_status: Deferred — requires full options surface data and sophisticated modeling

strategies.name: Dispersion / Correlation Trades
strategies.description: Exploits differences between index option pricing and single-name option pricing, especially around implied correlation and skew.
strategies.why_institutions_use_it: Implied correlation is systematically overpriced because index hedging demand inflates index vol relative to single-stock vol.
strategies.enso_status: Deferred — requires multi-leg execution, index + single-name options, significant capital

strategies.name: Event Vol Strangle
strategies.description: Systematically prices expected moves around earnings and catalysts. Buys vol when events are underpriced, sells when overpriced.
strategies.why_institutions_use_it: Event pricing is inefficient because retail tends to overpay for earnings protection, creating systematic selling opportunities around known catalysts.
strategies.enso_status: Ready to build — earnings calendars available, options data via Polygon

strategies.name: Term Structure Carry
strategies.description: Harvests roll yield from contango in VIX or equity vol term structure. Systematically short front-month vol when term structure is in contango.
strategies.why_institutions_use_it: VIX contango provides persistent positive carry, but catastrophic tail risk requires strict sizing and stop-losses.
strategies.enso_status: Phase 2 — needs VIX futures data or VIX ETP proxies

strategies.name: Pinning / 0DTE Dynamics
strategies.description: Identifies gamma pinning at high open interest strikes and trades around 0DTE hedging dynamics and expiration-day convergence.
strategies.why_institutions_use_it: 0DTE options now account for 50%+ of SPX volume. The hedging flows create predictable intraday patterns around key strikes.
strategies.enso_status: Phase 2 — needs intraday OI data and 0DTE flow tracking

strategies.name: Vol Risk Premium Harvest
strategies.description: Systematically sells options premium with strict filters. Only sells when IV exceeds RV by a safe margin, regime is not crisis, and skew is not extreme.
strategies.why_institutions_use_it: The volatility risk premium — the gap between what options cost and what markets actually move — is one of the most studied and persistent factors.
strategies.enso_status: Ready to build — natural extension of IV vs RV monitor with selling logic

strategies.name: Cross-Asset Signal Fusion
strategies.description: Combines equity vol, credit spreads, rate vol, and macro signals into regime labels (risk-on, risk-off, transition) that filter all other strategies.
strategies.why_institutions_use_it: Cross-asset signals catch regime shifts that single-market indicators miss. Credit widening before equity vol spikes is a classic early warning.
strategies.enso_status: Phase 3 — highest AI leverage but requires multi-source data pipeline

strategies.name: S/R + Vol Filter Overlay
strategies.description: Current Enso baseline. S/R pivot trading with confluence scoring. Adding vol context filters (IV vs RV, skew direction) improves signal quality.
strategies.why_institutions_use_it: Institutions rarely use pure S/R, but filtered S/R with vol context removes low-quality entries and improves risk-adjusted returns.
strategies.enso_status: LIVE — currently in production, ready for vol filter upgrade
